assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 for foreign currency risk on an intragroup dividend receivable after it has been recognised.
The recognised dividend receivable is treated as a monetary item exposed to foreign exchange risk.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to changes in fair value attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk associated with a recognised asset or liability or a highly probable forecast transaction.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately identifies hedge accounting for a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model in consolidated financial statements.
